jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer arriving home
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation after presumably crashing or having an accident
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated into opening sequence. While not original anymore, it's perfectly executed character comedy.

jokes.text: Swank. Ten times more addictive than marijuana.
jokes.setup: Movie villain revealing his evil drug creation
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous name 'Swank' for supposedly super-addictive drug
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the disconnect between the silly name and serious threat.

jokes.text: That is one evil dude. It's just a movie, son. There's nobody that evil in real life.
jokes.setup: Homer reassuring Bart about movie villain
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Burns doing sinister laugh, proving Homer wrong
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic juxtaposition with perfect timing. Classic Simpsons structural comedy.

jokes.text: Benefits, perks, a green cookie on St. Patrick's Day?
jokes.setup: Burns examining union contract benefits
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously minimal 'benefit' of one green cookie per year
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment showing his contempt for worker benefits through absurd minimalism.

jokes.text: Then we'll go too far and get corrupt and shiftless... and the Japanese will eat us alive!
jokes.setup: Worker predicting future of union organizing
jokes.punchline: Accurately predicting the decline of American manufacturing
jokes.explanation: Dark prophetic humor that works on multiple levels - both period comedy and economic satire.

jokes.text: The Japanese? Those sandal-wearing goldfish tenders? [Laughs] Bosh! Flimshaw!
jokes.setup: Past Burns dismissing Japanese as economic threat
jokes.punchline: Hilariously wrong prediction using pompous old-timey dismissals
jokes.explanation: Great historical irony with Burns's characteristic pompous language. 'Bosh! Flimshaw!' is perfectly quotable.

jokes.text: If only we had listened to that boy... instead of walling him up in the abandoned coke oven.
jokes.setup: Burns reflecting on past mistakes
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of murdering the prophetic worker
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with Burns treating murder as a minor business decision. Perfect character integration and structure.

jokes.text: How often do you brush, Ralph? Three times a day, sir. Why must you turn my office into a house of lies? You're right! I don't brush! [Crying] I don't brush.
jokes.setup: Dentist asking routine question about brushing
jokes.punchline: Ralph's immediate confession and emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum character moment with excellent escalation from lie to guilt to confession.

jokes.text: The Big Book of British Smiles
jokes.setup: Dentist showing educational book to scare child
jokes.punchline: Book title plays on stereotype of bad British dental hygiene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag title that became culturally quotable. Perfect economy and originality.

jokes.text: You know that rattle when you shake up spray paint? That's a kid's tooth.
jokes.setup: Child asking what dentist does with extracted teeth
jokes.punchline: Disturbing and absurd explanation about spray paint cans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist dark humor that's both disturbing and hilarious. Highly original concept.

jokes.text: Oh, no! I'll be socially unpopular. More so.
jokes.setup: Lisa worrying about braces affecting her popularity
jokes.punchline: Dentist's brutal honesty that she's already unpopular
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with brutal economy. Classic cruel-but-funny moment.

jokes.text: Cool! She'll be a freak. We can stick her in a trailer, drive her around the South and charge two bits a gander.
jokes.setup: Family seeing Lisa's future dental projection
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately seeing carnival sideshow opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - completely inappropriate but totally in character entrepreneurial spirit.

jokes.text: Where's my burrito? Where's my burrito? Where's my burrito? Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer reminiscing about past strike
jokes.punchline: Repetitive demand ending in pain
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny repetitive gag that's more character color than strong comedy.

jokes.text: Dental plan! Lisa needs braces. Dental plan! Lisa needs braces.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to focus during union meeting
jokes.punchline: His mind cycling between the two connected thoughts
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment showing Homer's thought process. Became hugely quotable and meme-worthy. Perfect character writing.

jokes.text: Bull's-eye! Thanks a lot, Carl. Now I've lost my train of thought.
jokes.setup: Homer's important realization interrupted by dart game
jokes.punchline: His frustration at losing the crucial connection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's distractibility at a crucial moment.

jokes.text: Yoink!
jokes.setup: Homer grabbing Lenny's diamond tooth
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature sound effect for grabbing things
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer sound effect that became part of cultural lexicon. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Well, I wouldn't have old chomper here, that's for sure.
jokes.setup: Discussion about dental plan benefits
jokes.punchline: Gummy Joe affectionately referring to his single tooth
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment with endearing name for his lone tooth.

jokes.text: Actually, sir, he thwarted your campaign for governor... you ran over his son, he saved the plant from meltdown... and his wife painted you in the nude-- Eh, doesn't ring a bell.
jokes.setup: Smithers listing Homer's major interactions with Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns's complete inability to remember despite massive events
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback joke that rewards long-time viewers while showing Burns's character trait.

jokes.text: Hey, what does this job pay? Nothin'. D'oh! Unless you're crooked. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about union president salary
jokes.punchline: Homer's mood swing from disappointment to excitement about corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment with his signature sound effects and moral flexibility.

jokes.text: And make lifelong connections to the world of organized crime. Hmm. Organized crime.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to be supportive about union presidency
jokes.punchline: Homer fixating on the wrong part of her encouragement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's tendency to focus on wrong details.

jokes.text: Don Homer, I have baked a special donut just for you. Mmm. Grazie.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony approaching Homer as new union boss
jokes.punchline: Mafia don offering donut instead of money
jokes.explanation: Nice absurdist touch with mafia corruption taking the form of baked goods.

jokes.text: Dad, I'll trade you this delicious doorstop for your crummy old Danish. Done and done! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Lisa offering to trade food with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately agreeing despite her calling it a doorstop, then realizing
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Homer's poor negotiating skills and Lisa's intelligence.

jokes.text: These braces are invisible, painless... and periodically release a delightful burst... of Calvin Klein's Obsession for Teeth.
jokes.setup: Dentist describing expensive braces
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous luxury feature of perfume for teeth
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the parody of luxury branding applied to dental work.

jokes.text: These predate stainless steel... so you can't get them wet.
jokes.setup: Need for affordable braces
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously primitive braces with major flaw
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist joke about the impracticality of cheap alternatives.

jokes.text: Hey, Lenny, can you get this Sugar Daddy off my back? Okay, but it's the last time.
jokes.setup: Workers at candy machine during strike discussion
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'Sugar Daddy' candy stuck to Carl's back
jokes.explanation: Clever visual pun playing on candy name and colloquial expression.

jokes.text: Why is Mr. Burns being so nice to me? Oh, my God! He is coming on to me. [Silent Scream]
jokes.setup: Burns using business metaphors about negotiations
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misinterpreting as sexual advances
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy from Homer's misunderstanding of business idioms. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Sorry, Mr. Burns, but I don't go in for these backdoor shenanigans. Sure, I'm flattered, maybe even a little curious, but the answer is no.
jokes.setup: Homer's misunderstanding of Burns's business talk
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward rejection of imagined sexual advances
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy with Homer's complete misreading of the situation. The 'curious' part adds perfect character touch.

jokes.text: This is the scraper, this is the poker... and this happy little fellow is the gouger.
jokes.setup: Dentist showing dental tools to scared child
jokes.punchline: Sinister tools described with cheerful, friendly language
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with the contrast between menacing tools and cheerful presentation.

jokes.text: Look, fellas! It's Lisa in the sky! No diamonds though. Look out for the campy drawing of Queen Victoria!
jokes.setup: Lisa hallucinating under dental gas
jokes.punchline: Beatles reference combined with British dental stereotype
jokes.explanation: Decent reference humor tying back to earlier British dental joke.

jokes.text: Goons. Who? Hired goons. Hired goons?
jokes.setup: Doorbell ring at Simpson house
jokes.punchline: Professional goons politely announcing themselves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment with the casual politeness of criminal intimidators. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You know, you could have just called me. Oh, yes, but the telephone is so impersonal. I prefer the hands-on touch you only get with hired goons.
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out Burns could have just phoned
jokes.punchline: Burns treating goon intimidation as customer service preference
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with Burns's characteristic twisted logic about criminal methods.

jokes.text: ¡Ay, un gato malodoro!
jokes.setup: Burns showing off his large TV
jokes.punchline: Spanish exclamation about a 'smelly cat' on massive screen
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag that's mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It was the best of times, it was the 'blurst' of times? You stupid monkey!
jokes.setup: Thousand monkeys typing supposed to produce great literature
jokes.punchline: Almost perfect except for one wrong letter making nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of infinite monkey theorem. 'Blurst' became culturally iconic. Perfect structure and economy.

jokes.text: Gee, it's not as nice as the other rooms. Yes, I really should stop ending the tour with it.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Burns's terrible basement
jokes.punchline: Burns's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of poor tour planning
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan exchange showing Burns's social obliviousness

jokes.text: Why did I have all that beer and coffee and watermelon? It doesn't take a whiz to see that you're looking out for number one. Well, listen to me, and you'll make a big splash very soon.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately needs bathroom during Burns's speech
jokes.punchline: Burns unknowingly using bathroom-related puns
jokes.explanation: Good layered comedy with Homer's discomfort and Burns's oblivious bathroom puns.

jokes.text: Simpson, be a dear and rub my legs till the feeling comes back. A bug, I tell you! A bug!
jokes.setup: Burns falling into basement trap
jokes.punchline: Burns's physical vulnerability and continued threats
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Burns's frailty contrasted with his megalomania.

jokes.text: Guys are always patting my bald head for luck... pinchin' my belly to hear my girlish laugh. That doesn't sound like they like you at all. You're right. First thing tomorrow, I'm gonna punch Lenny in the back of the head.
jokes.setup: Homer describing what he thinks are signs of affection
jokes.punchline: Complete misunderstanding leading to plan for violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer character moment showing his complete social misreading and solution through violence.

jokes.text: I bet you've got a beautiful smile. Why don't you share it with the world? [Gasps] There is no God!
jokes.setup: School photographer encouraging Lisa to smile
jokes.punchline: Lisa's horrific braces causing existential crisis in photographer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect setup/payoff structure. The existential horror is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Who keeps saying that? It was him. Let's get him, fellas.
jokes.setup: Repeated 'Nay' votes during union meetings
jokes.punchline: Finally identifying and pursuing the dissenter
jokes.explanation: Decent payoff to running gag of the lone dissenter.

jokes.text: We can't bust heads like we used to... but we have our ways. One trick is to tell them stories that don't go anywhere...
jokes.setup: Old strikebreakers discussing their methods
jokes.punchline: Beginning of the infamous rambling story technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that's about to demonstrate exactly what it describes. Highly original concept.

jokes.text: So I tied an onion to my belt... which was the style at the time. Give me five bees for a quarter, you'd say. The important thing was that I had an onion on my belt... which was the style at the time.
jokes.setup: Grampa's rambling strikebreaking story
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd historical details and circular repetition
jokes.explanation: Legendary rambling story that became one of the most quoted Simpsons bits. Perfect absurdist character comedy that demonstrates its own premise.

jokes.text: 'Give me five bees for a quarter,' you'd say.
jokes.setup: Continuing the bee nickel story
jokes.punchline: Absurd currency exchange with bees
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of the absurd currency system - 'five bees for a quarter' is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Homer, organized labor has been called a lumbering dinosaur. [Screams] My director is telling me not to talk to you anymore.
jokes.setup: TV interview question about unions
jokes.punchline: Homer's reaction suggests he's literally a dinosaur in visual
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with Homer's appearance apparently being dinosaur-like enough to scare the interviewer.

jokes.text: Fifteen minutes from now, I will wreak a terrible vengeance on this city! No one will be spared! No one!
jokes.setup: Burns given platform for opening tirade
jokes.punchline: Using TV time to make villainous threats
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment with his complete lack of media awareness and pure villainy.

jokes.text: They sing without juicers. They sing without blenders. They sing without flunjers, capdabblers and smendlers!
jokes.setup: Burns observing Springfield's resilience during blackout
jokes.punchline: Dr. Seuss parody with made-up kitchen appliance names
jokes.explanation: Clever Dr. Seuss parody with inventive nonsense words that sound plausible.

jokes.text: Smithers, I'm beginning to think that Homer Simpson... was not the brilliant tactician I thought he was.
jokes.setup: Homer's quick capitulation to Burns's demands
jokes.punchline: Burns's massive understatement about Homer's negotiating skills
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor with Burns's dry observation about Homer's obvious lack of skill.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, you can hardly see your new braces! And that's the tooth!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Lisa's barely visible new braces
jokes.punchline: Terrible tooth/truth pun to end the episode
jokes.explanation: Weak closing pun that's more groan-worthy than funny. Classic bad dad joke territory.

jokes.text: Oops. I left the gas on.
jokes.setup: Episode seemingly ending happily
jokes.punchline: Lisa still under effects of dental gas, suggesting episode was hallucination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twist ending that recontextualizes the entire episode. Perfect economy and structure for a final joke.
